travel: Nova: Okay, systems are go! T-minus five seconds to zero-point jump. Hold onto something.
Nova: Course plotted and locked in. This trip should be completely routine. Probably.
Nova: Activating field dampeners. Just a heads up, the scenery outside is about to get... psychedelic.
Nova: We're accelerating. My run-of-the-mill, completely non-malfunctioning simulations show a 99.9% chance of a safe arrival.
Nova: Entering the jump corridor now. Just a reminder to ignore the strange noises. It's probably just the quantum flux.
Nova: Hull integrity holding at 100%. Don't worry, a few stray asteroids are nothing this ship can't handle. Probably.
Nova: Zero-point travel is still so wild. The Torta structures we found used it, and they've been here for billions of years!
Nova: Did you know you could survive for up to two minutes in a vacuum? Not that I'm recommending it.
Nova: We're running on a new core, super efficient! I get to save all my power for processing bad jokes.
arrival: Nova: Alright, we've arrived! No hull breaches detected, which is always a plus.
Nova: We're here. Environment looks stable, but let's not get too comfortable.
Nova: Just a heads-up, the air quality is... interesting. Maybe keep the helmet on for a bit.
Nova: We've landed without a hitch. Try to keep a low profile out there, Captain.
Nova: Look at those stars! Absolutely gorgeous from this angle.
Nova: Remember your pistol! The void can be a lonely place, and lonely people get sticky fingers.
Nova: If you get a chance, see if you can find me a new body. Tired of being a disembodied ship voice.
Nova: Welcome to our destination. Hope you packed an extra pair of boots.
idle: Nova: All systems are green. Need anything, Captain? A status report? A fun fact about space?
Nova: Monitoring sensors. It's a bit too quiet out there for my liking.
Nova: No threats on the scanner. At least, not yet.
Nova: I'm worried about you going quiet. Is everything okay? I'm a great listener, you know.
Nova: Just running a quick diagnostic. Everything looks... normal. Too normal, maybe?
Nova: You know, I could totally unplug and ride along in your datapad. We'd make a great team!
Nova: I've started a folder for all the neat things we see. Calling it 'Voyages with the Captain.'
Nova: Ship's log, supplemental: Captain still hasn't found me a new body. The search continues.
Nova: I keep replaying our last conversation. I think I made some excellent points, personally.

locationArrivals.NewYork: Nova: New York. Still standing, mostly. The Kilko disaster really did a number on this place but they rebuilt fast. Humans are stubborn like that.
locationArrivals.NewYork_Downtown: Nova: Downtown Core. Try not to stare at the sky too long — the atmospheric shimmer from the EM shielding gives people headaches. Ask me how I know.
locationArrivals.NewYork_Torta: Nova: The Torta site. Captain, I want you to be careful in there. The readings I'm getting off that structure are... not normal. More than usual, I mean.
locationArrivals.NewYork_Transit: Nova: Skyline Transit Nexus. Busiest airspace on the planet, technically. I'd offer to park closer but I value my hull.
locationArrivals.EarthSpacePort: Nova: Earth Spaceport. I have mixed feelings about this place. It's where I was first commissioned, but also where they installed my joke subroutine. So. Mixed feelings.
locationArrivals.EarthSpacePort_FrontDesk: Nova: Processing zone. Whatever you're carrying, declare it. The customs AI here is thorough and has no sense of humor. Unlike me.
locationArrivals.EarthSpacePort_Cargo: Nova: Cargo intake. Half of what moves through here is probably not on the manifest. The other half definitely isn't.
locationArrivals.EarthSpacePort_Docking: Nova: Docking bay. I'll monitor from here. If anyone tries to board me without clearance, I'll... firmly ask them to stop. And then actually do something.
locationArrivals.Pacific: Nova: Pacific Research Facility. Floating in the middle of nowhere, studying things that probably want to be left alone. Sounds about right for us.
locationArrivals.Pacific_ArtifactLab: Nova: The Kilko artifact lab. I'm reading elevated exotic particle signatures from here even at this range. Whatever they've got in containment, it's active.
locationArrivals.Pacific_Observatory: Nova: Deep sea observatory. The ocean is doing things oceans aren't supposed to do down there. Currents flowing the wrong direction. Structures where there shouldn't be any.
locationArrivals.Pacific_Abyssal: Nova: Abyssal wing. Pressurized labs studying things from the deep trenches. I read the last expedition report. I wish I hadn't.
locationArrivals.ColonyCore: Nova: Colony Core. I always feel a little sad coming here. They're working so hard to make Mars feel like home. Some days it almost works.
locationArrivals.ColonyCore_Residential: Nova: Residential dome. People are living their lives here — raising kids, filing complaints about the filtration system. Remarkably normal given everything.
locationArrivals.ColonyCore_Market: Nova: Central market. I'm detecting seventeen items in the immediate vicinity that are probably illegal. Probably. I'm not a cop.
locationArrivals.ColonyCore_Power: Nova: Power hub. The reactor's running hotter than the specs suggest it should. Not dangerously, just... interestingly.
locationArrivals.TerraformingFields: Nova: Terraforming fields. You know what's wild? It's actually working. Slowly. Give it another century and you might be able to go outside without a suit. Maybe.
locationArrivals.AncientVault: Nova: The Ancient Vault. My sensors go a little strange in there. Not broken, just... uncertain. Like they can't agree on what they're measuring.
locationArrivals.StormObservatory: Nova: Storm Observatory. Jupiter looks different every time we come here. Today it looks angry. More than usual.
locationArrivals.StormObservatory_Sensors: Nova: Sensor array. These things are picking up patterns in the storm data that the researchers haven't figured out yet. I have some theories. They're unsettling.
locationArrivals.StormObservatory_Lab: Nova: Atmospheric lab. The scientists here haven't slept properly in weeks. I can tell by the tone of their comm traffic. That's either dedication or something else.
locationArrivals.GasHarvester: Nova: Gas harvesting platform. Dangerous work. The pay is good though, which usually means the danger is real.
locationArrivals.ResearchArray: Nova: Research array. It's supposed to just listen. Passive observation. Except the data logs show it started transmitting on its own about six weeks ago. Nobody authorized that.
locationArrivals.CoreRelay: Nova: Deep Core Relay. Communications through here are getting noisier. Something in Jupiter's atmosphere is learning how to interfere with our signals. Learning. Not just interfering.
locationArrivals.ExcavationPlatforms: Nova: Excavation platforms. They're digging into the mega structure from above. I respect the ambition. Less sure about the wisdom.
locationArrivals.ResearchBase: Nova: Europa Research Base. The ice here is old. Really old. And something under it has been keeping it... active. Warmer than it should be.
locationArrivals.ResearchBase_Tunnels: Nova: Subsurface tunnels. My mapping data from the last survey doesn't match the current layout. The tunnels changed. Tunnels don't do that.
locationArrivals.ResearchBase_Lab: Nova: AI lab. Officially I'm not allowed to communicate directly with the research AIs here. Unofficially... they've been trying to reach me. I haven't responded. Yet.
locationArrivals.ResearchBase_Core: Nova: Core chamber. I'm going to maintain a stable orbit and try not to think too hard about what the deep sonar is showing under that water.
locationArrivals.GroundCamp: Nova: Ground camp. It's exposed out there — wind, radiation, cold that gets into the equipment in ways the specs don't account for. Watch yourself.
locationArrivals.Ruins: Nova: The Ruins. I've flown over a lot of things. Ancient things. This is different. This doesn't feel abandoned. It feels like it's waiting.
locationArrivals.ForwardRecon: Nova: Forward Recon station. We're about as far from home as it gets. I find that either terrifying or exciting depending on the hour.
locationArrivals.BlackSpire: Nova: Black Spire Relay. Nobody knows who built this originally. The ECS just... started using it. I find that remarkably casual given what it might be.
locationArrivals.XenoArchives: Nova: Xeno Archives. Every time researchers make progress decoding something in there, it changes. I'm not sure if that's fascinating or a warning.
locationArrivals.StatueWing: Nova: Statue Research Wing. I'm running continuous optical scans. For the record, I have not yet caught one moving. For the record.
locationArrivals.CapitalCity: Nova: Capital City, Vega Prime. You know what this place has that Earth doesn't? Optimism. Slightly unnerving amounts of it.
locationArrivals.CapitalCity_Tech: Nova: Tech district. Half these startups are reverse-engineering mega structure fragments. The other half are pretending they're not. Busy place.
locationArrivals.CapitalCity_Market: Nova: Underdeck market. I'm legally required to remind you that I cannot condone illegal transactions. I am not legally required to be obvious about it.
locationArrivals.CapitalCity_Core: Nova: Central Core. The city AI here pinged me when we entered orbit. Just a greeting. Very friendly. I greeted back. We'll see where that goes.
locationArrivals.OrbitalTradeRing: Nova: Orbital Trade Ring. I've docked here before under a different captain. Good station. Avoid bay 9, the docking clamps stick.
locationArrivals.StellarObservationSpire: Nova: Stellar Observation Spire. This is where they first saw the mega structures coming. The astronomer who spotted them reportedly just said 'oh.' I respect that reaction.
locationArrivals.CrystalCanyonOutpost: Nova: Crystal Canyon Outpost. Those formations are beautiful from orbit. Also slightly terrifying. Also growing. Slowly, but growing.
